TextENGNew:
"Listen up, everyone.
If you don't take cooking seriously, someone will get hurt.
This is a class, so please pay attention and give it your best!"
changeReason: Old translates ふざけないでしっかりやって下さい as 'don't fool around and do it seriously'; new softens to 'please pay attention and give it your best.'

TextENGNew: The whole class answered with a cheerful "yeaaah."
changeReason: Old describes the class response as 'lazy'; new says 'cheerful,' matching the Japanese 陽気に応える (respond cheerfully). Opposite tone.

TextENGNew: Half the school day today was devoted to home economics.
The plan was for everyone to make curry rice and serve it to the forest rangers who let us use their building as a school.

If everyone cooked in one pot it would be a party, but this was class!
Leaving the younger students aside, each of us would make our own curry and be graded on the result.

"Now then, everyone.
Curry may seem like an easy dish anyone can make these days, but it is in fact a venerable cuisine rooted in ancient India."

"Though it has been adapted to the Japanese palate, it still carries the wisdom and culture of ancient India within it.
Do not forget that fact."
"Anyone who doesn't take this seriously will have words with me — consider yourselves warned."
...That was a needlessly passionate lecture on curry. ...Well, whatever.

"It's said that Chie-sensei has devoted her life to curry.
She'll probably be a strict judge."
"As long as you're not failing, your grade in home economics doesn't really matter. Doesn't matter at all."

"You shouldn't say things like that...
The teacher turns into a demon when curry is involved."

"...What does that even mean? A curry demon?
...Never heard that before."

"She's normally a gentle, good-natured teacher, you know.
...For some reason curry is the one thing she's strict about!
Oh, passionately so!!"

"Yeah.
She said she travels to India every year to research secret curry recipes."
"...The rumour goes that she eats curry rice three times a day, and even when she occasionally has something else, it always has to have a curry flavour."

"...That's not obsession, that's a disorder.
Is she in some kind of cult...?"
changeReason: Old translates 偏執的/変態的 as 'unyielding/unnatural'; new uses 'obsession/disorder' — the Japanese 変態的 means 'perverted/abnormal' making 'disorder' closer to the intended meaning.

TextENGNew: "Besides, isn't curry basically the king of junk food? Curry three times a day is nutritionally unbalanced and—"
*THUNK THUNK THUNK!!*
A chef's knife, a ladle, and a spatula had embedded themselves in the table right at my fingertips and were still quivering.
The teacher, still stirring her own pot, was looking our way with a smile.

"K-Kei-chan, shhhhhh!!
...Say one more word and you'll be buried...!!"
Saying any more would make things considerably worse for me.

"Yeah, yeah.
...I just have to be serious and make the curry properly, right?"
"...I don't think today will be that simple."
"You see... Mii-chan is..."
...I could see exactly where this was going.
...On equal ground with rankings at stake... there was no way this wouldn't turn into a contest!
"Looks like we're all thinking the same thing!!"
"It's a cooking contest!!"

I knew it!!!
There was no way Mion would let an event this tempting pass by!

"How do we determine the winner?
By the teacher's score?"
"But wait, there's more" — and appearing to say those very words were the teacher and the principal.
changeReason: Old is truncated with only the opening phrase; new provides the complete sentence identifying the teacher and principal who appeared.

TextENGNew: "Since today's event is to show our appreciation to the Forestry Service, the judging will be done by everyone together, not just me."

"Indeed. Your teacher and I, along with five people from the Forestry Service, will be your judges!
May each of you do your very best!!
Gahahaha!!!"
E-Even the principal?!?!
Looking around, I could see the whole class watching with keen interest.

"That's right.
This won't end as some casual curry party!!"

"Rena is good at curry!
I won't lose today~!"
"My grade is working in groups, so I'm with Rika."

"Th-That's unfair!!
If I remember right, Rika-chan is quite a good cook!"
"...My curry will send a shiver right through you."
What about Mion, then?
She didn't look like she'd be any good at cooking.

Her type has always been clumsy at this sort of thing.
...But Mion wore the same fearless expression as always.

"Hehehe...
...I'll tell you exactly what Kei-chan is thinking right now.
...'Mion must be terrible at cooking.' ...Am I right~?"
I... didn't like the way she said that.
...No way... surely not Mion of all people...
At that moment the teacher's whistle sounded.

"All right, everyone, are you ready?
Be very careful with the knives.
Begin~!!"
The battle had begun.
We had to cook the rice in a camping canister, but that wasn't difficult for someone who'd done plenty of day-camping.
Wash the rice, put it in the canister, press your palm to the bottom... fill with water to just above the wrist.

"Are you sure that's enough water?
I heard you're supposed to add more?"
Liar.
Add any more and I'll end up with half-cooked porridge!

"Satoko, if that's supposed to be a trick, you're out of luck.
I know my way around a camp canister!
Start low, then turn it up.
And don't lift the lid no matter what!"

"Wow, Keiichi-kun, that's impressive!
That's a little unexpected.
Did you learn it at summer camp?"

"My dad loves day-camping.
We go out a lot as a family in the summer."

"So that means... everyone is fine with cooking the rice.
In other words, the curry is what decides the winner!"

"Hmph!
Curry is Rika's domain!
We'll crush all of you!!"

"...The part about 'Rika's' is what gets me.
Why don't you help with the cooking too?"
"Hmm, maybe... Satoko-chan's greatest contribution is doing nothing at all☆."

"Eep!!!
The winner is always justified, so I just have to win!!!"
That was a surprisingly pointed comment from Rena.
Satoko went after her for it, while Rena ran away laughing and apologising.

"Well then... shall we move on to the curry?
This is where the real battle begins!"
After rinsing the carrots, potatoes, and other standard vegetables at the sink, I picked up the knife. ...Gulp.
"What will today's curry taste like~♪"
Rena hummed along as her hands worked the knife with practised ease.

The rhythmic thud of the blade against the cutting board called to mind a gentler era.
...In plain terms: it was perfect.
She was so calm and assured... I had no chance against her.
I glanced over at Satoko and Rika-chan.
...Satoko was focused on washing the vegetables, while Rika-chan was in charge of peeling them.
...I'd heard about it, but Rika-chan's knife skills were something else entirely!!
She turned the potato in her fingers, peeling it in one smooth, cheerful motion.

What's more... the peel came off in one long strip.
Katsuramuki, wasn't it called?
I'd heard that peeling a vegetable in one unbroken spiral was considered a proper technique.
She plucked the peel from the sink and carved a pair of eyes into it with the tip of her knife...
"...It's a snake. Roaaaar."
Saying so, she draped the long potato peel over the head of a boy from her group.

...She was utterly at ease.
...For Rika-chan, cooking wasn't even a contest.

...But... roaaaar?
Is that the sound snakes make?
Now then, how was Mion doing?
She had to be fumbling through it!

There was no way she could peel a potato that smoothly!
Absolutely no way!!

"This can't be real!!
It's a lie!
She's using tricks! Special effects!
There must be a wire somewhere — she's using a wire!"

"Hehehehe...
Here, see for yourself."
With the air of someone who had already won, Mion held out the long unbroken potato peel in triumph.

"...You didn't know, Keiichi?
Mii was taught how to cook by her grandmother."

"Not just cooking~.
From sewing to flower arranging to koto — to firearms, radio operation, even helicopter piloting, there's nothing she can't do!"
...Strangely, the last items on that list were the more believable ones.
It was the sewing and flower arranging at the start that sounded like a lie.

"She's actually a wonderful cook.
...She just finds it too much trouble to bother~."
D-Dammit!!

I'm a good cook too, you know...!
Especially Chinese food!!
"...Keiichi, boiling water and pouring it into a bowl doesn't count as cooking."
She shot down the very thing I was about to say before I could open my mouth.

"Oh ho ho!!
How the mighty have fallen!!
Today, Keiichi-san is assured of losing all on his own~~~!!!"
*clonk!!!*
"Tough talk from someone who can't even cook herself!!!"

"Waaaaaaaa!!!
Rikaaaaa~!"

"There there.
...I'll thrash Keiichi for you."
Gah! ...The odds were stacked against me...!
Everyone was smirking at me!
Don't raise the white flag yet, Keiichi Maebara!!
Don't throw this match away!
Think clearly!

Not about how to peel potatoes well.
...About how to win this contest!!

"...There are too many eyes on me here.
I'm going to work at another sink."
Saying only that, I gathered my vegetables and left.

"Will Keiichi-kun be all right?
I'm worried he might cut himself with the knife..."
"Let's see what Kei-chan manages~!"
If I tried to peel at that speed, the most likely outcome was lopping off a finger!
Then... there was only one thing to do!!

"Tomita-kun, Okamura-kun!
Oh, you two are in a group of four, aren't you."
These were the two underclassmen who had sworn brotherhood with me at that toy store event last Sunday.
It seemed they had been paired with two girls to form a group of four.

"Yes.
Both the girls are pretty good at cooking."
"So the two of us can basically just mess around."
...Both of them pointed to the girls.
...Not quite Rena's level, but their knife work was more than sufficient.

"I'll come straight to the point.
At this rate I'm going to lose the contest!
Give me the vegetables your group has already peeled."

"M-Maebara-san... that's practically extortion.
...But, depending on the terms..."
changeReason: Old translates 恐喝 as 'theft'; new correctly translates as 'extortion,' matching the Japanese meaning.

TextENGNew: "It all comes down to negotiation."

"Hmph, bold of you. ...Well, that's to be expected.
Then let's do this."
"If I win, I'll let you eat the curry from Satoko and Rika-chan's group. What do you say?"
"Eh... ah... I-I'm not sure about that..."
Both of them were rattled by the weight of that offer... but it still seemed one nudge short.
Very well then — time for the clincher.
...I pulled them both close by the shoulders and spoke in a low voice.
"...And by curry, I mean what Satoko and Rika-chan have already eaten from."
"Spoons included."

*SPLAT*
A red spray erupted vigorously from the noses of both underclassmen.

"Oh...?
...Keiichi-san... is actually quite good at this...!
Those are some very fine potatoes."
I had cleared the vegetable-peeling stage brilliantly and returned to my comrades in triumph.

"Whoa, amazing, amazing!
Keiichi-kun's peeling is really good!"

"...Hmph.
That's not quite right.
What Kei-chan has bared isn't a potato — it's his fangs."

"Yeah...
I've been serious for a while now.
I'm going to crush every last one of you!!"

Prep done, I brought the pot to the boil and added the vegetables in order of cooking time.
...I'd learned this much from my mother on camping trips.
But... going through the motions from here on out... probably wouldn't earn many points.
Glancing at Rena... she had a variety of vegetables simmering together in a beautifully balanced way.

There was no ambition in it, no game-playing calculation.
...It was the technique of a mother who simply wanted to make something delicious.

I had a feeling I could somehow keep pace with Mion on technical grounds, but against Rena I didn't stand a chance.
...She was in a completely different league.

"When my mother made curry, she always put in lots of ingredients.
So Rena's curry has lots of ingredients too.
...I really wanted to let it simmer slowly overnight, though... hau."

"...I'm begging you.
Won't you spare just a little of your skill for my curry..."

"Ahahahaha!
No~.
This is a club activity!
Rena won't lose~!"
Ugh, once club mode was engaged, even the normally kind Rena became an enemy...

It looked like Rika-chan was adding apples to the pot as well.
...Urgh... she was like a professional.
How was Mion doing, then?

"Wha — Mion, you—!!
What is that?!
Where did the hamburger steak come from!!"

"I checked the schedule in advance so I knew what today would involve.
...So I prepared accordingly.
...My curry is going to be magnificent~!!"
Looking over... Mion had spread a huge assortment of vegetables, spices, and ingredients she had brought from home across the whole counter!!
She... had hauled all of this from home just for today!!!

"I-I object!!!
Chie-sensei, this has to be against the rules!!"

"Objection overruled.
As long as it makes for delicious curry, anything goes☆."
Ugh... for that normally strict teacher to append a star to the end of her sentence!!
...Just as the rumours said... a dyed-in-the-wool curry fanatic...

"Rena and Rika-chan are natural-born cooks!
I came with thorough preparations!!
...And?
Kei-chan, what about you?
Hehehehe...!!"

...It was hopeless...
In the full scope of cooking... peeling the vegetables was only one small part of it.
...The hurdles I still had to clear were far too many... and far too high!!
"...Maebara-san, over here!"
Turning around, I saw the voices belonged to the two underclassmen from earlier, Tomita-kun and Okamura-kun.

"What's up, you two?
...Hmm?
What's that pot? Is it from your group?"

"Swap this out with your pot before anyone notices.
...I think the curry our girls made is at least better than what you could manage."
...Y-You guys... ...for my sake...!?

"D-Don't get the wrong idea.
We need you to win, otherwise... well..."
Right, of course.
If I didn't win, the deal about eating Satoko and Rika-chan's curry was off the table!!
It seemed these two had found their fangs as well...!

"Thank you!!
I owe you one!!
...But I'm surprised the girls in your group actually agreed to this."

"Maebara-san.
...We're serious about this too!"

Three days of class-duty coverage.
...These two... to secure my victory... had paid the price!!!
That burning gaze from my underclassmen...!!
I wasn't fighting this alone.
...We were all fighting together!!!

"Leave it to me!!!!
Keep your stomachs empty.
I'm definitely going to let you eat your fill!!!"
"We shall be eagerly awaiting your success!!"
Gas burners at least made the heat easy to control.

All that remained was to let it stew for a while.
The rich smell of curry began to drift upward...
The pot my two underclassmen had handed over was certainly impressive.
Even Rena looked surprised when she came over to check on it, so it had to be well above average.

"...Kei-chan, you're not bad.
This looks like it'll be anyone's match right to the very end!"
"Rena wants to eat Keiichi-kun's curry too. Wants to eat it~!"

"Rena's curry and Mion's curry alike... you're all formidable opponents.
I've done my very best... but as for the outcome..."

"Keiichi-san, Rika is calling for you.
Go and see what she wants."

Huh?
Rika-chan's calling me?
What could it be?
...Well, whatever. While I'm at it I can scope out the enemy.
Rika-chan was crouched in front of her pot, doodling on the ground.

...All that was left for her was to let it simmer.
...Even so, not leaving the stove unattended was admirably thorough.
"Hey, Rika-chan, how's yours coming along?"
"...It's Mr. Curry."

...Huh?
...Rika-chan was drawing something strange on the ground and introduced it as Mr. Curry.

"...Mr. Curry is amazing.
He fires beams from his eyes.
...Pewwww."
Apparently Mr. Curry fires beams from his eyes.

She drew the beam with a stick, tracing a line toward my feet.
...Did that mean I had been hit?

"Uwhoaaaaaa!!
Barrier!!
Beam reflect!!!"
I grabbed a stick of my own, deflected the beam, and sent it straight back into Mr. Curry!!

"...Mr. Curry can also fire missiles from his belly.
...Psheewww."

"Hoooaaaahhh!!
Maebara Super Electromagnetic Barrier!!!
And Retaliatory Beam!!!"
"...Mr. Curry absorbs the beam energy and fires his Wave Cannon."

Rika-chan and I scratched at the ground and were thoroughly engrossed in our doodle war.
...But wait.

"Hey, Rika-chan.
...You called me here for a reason, didn't you?"

"...Yes, I did call you.
...But my purpose has already been fulfilled."

At that moment a chill ran up my spine.
...Already... fulfilled her purpose!?
"They — they got me!!!!!!!"
I sprinted back to my pot... and standing there, just as I feared, was Satoko!!!
Without a word I dropkicked the back of her head!

"Wh-What do you think you're doing!!!
You know better than to do that to a lady's head!!!"

"That's beside the point!
Satoko, did you mix something into my curry—!!"

"How rude.
...I haven't sunk that low.
...Despite appearances, I do have a sense of fair play."

"...Then what is this incomprehensible arrangement of ladles and plates?!
Left in positions that unstable — what were you planning if they fell over?!"
At that moment a gentle breeze stirred, and a ladle that had been leant upright toppled over.
Like a domino, the ladle knocked into the next utensil, which knocked into the next.

*thwap thwap thwap thwap*
*clang clang*
*crash crash*

"This is art.
...Come to think of it, domino world-record attempts were quite the craze on television a while back."

"K-Kei-chan!!!
The frying pan!!!"
I didn't even have time to react.
...The chain reaction knocked over a cutting board... then a frying pan... and the next thing after that was... my pot...
*CRASHHHHH...!!*
The frying pan, using its full weight, sent my pot flying!!
Its contents were mercilessly scattered across the ground... my carefully crafted curry was now being sampled by the schoolyard.
I stood there, utterly dumbfounded.
The trap was so beautifully engineered that for a moment I lost touch with reality.

"Oh my oh my, what a catastrophe~!
I didn't do a thing, you know?
Ooohohohoho!!"
...At the very last moment... I had let my guard down...!!
I had been far too careless with Satoko, who couldn't cook...!!
Even if she couldn't cook, Satoko still had this method of entering the fray!
Just then, Rena, who had been sampling her own pot, let out a shriek.

"Wh-Whaaaaat?!?!
Rena's curry is... salty? Salty!?"

So Rena had already been sabotaged as well...?!
What about Mion...!?

"My rice is saltyyyy!!
I've been had!!!"

"Ooohohohoho!!!
With this, every obstacle has been swept aside before the battle even begins!!"

"...All that effort, wasted.
So sad, so sad."
"So very very sad~."
Rika-chan and Satoko rubbed my dumbfounded head to their hearts' content.
The desks were pushed together and the time for judging had finally arrived.
The delicious scent of curry spread throughout the courtyard.
The members of the Forestry Service, to whom we owed so much, were guided to the guests' seats.
The principal gave a brief ceremonial address. Then the teacher asked the visitors to please judge the curry contest.
The men from the Forestry Service seemed to enjoy that sort of thing and gave their enthusiastic approval.

"Well then, is everyone finished~?
All the judges will now sample your cooking~!"
Everyone in the class plated their carefully made curry and lined it up on the table.
The only members of the club who had managed a decent result were Rika-chan's group.

Knowing full well it was a failure, Rena plated her curry for the judges regardless.
She already knew the score would be low.
But... at least she was still able to serve hers.
Both Mion's and my curry had been completely destroyed, so we couldn't even put ours on the table.
The judges started their tasting from the curry made by the younger grades.
They had an animated discussion, praising the entries.
And finally... it was our turn.

"Next is Ryuugu-kun's curry.
Yes.
...This looks quite appetising!!"
The principal cheerfully shovelled Rena's oversalted curry into his mouth...!!
Of course, his expression immediately changed.

"Oh my... what happened, Ryuugu-san?
It seemed so promising while you were cooking it..."
She must have had high hopes; the teacher's disappointment was clear.

"Hau... I-I'm sorry...
I may have accidentally added a whole bag of salt..."

The other judges grimaced after a single taste.
...My sympathies.

"Failure is but the seasoning for success!!
I look forward to next time!
Do your best!!"
Saying that, the principal devoured the rest of the oversalted curry in one go. Truly a man among men.
Rena was down.
Her offensive capability had been impressive, but her undoing was a weak defence.

"...Well then.
Please savour ours next."

"Furude-san's group has clearly put in a great deal of effort!
Let me see here.
...Hmm.
Simple, but very nicely done☆."
After the disaster of Rena's salted curry, the judges' assessment was extraordinarily favourable.
Not just extraordinarily — this might well be the best thing they'd had all day.
The judges launched into a spirited debate.
In the midst of it, one of them sighed and pushed a chunk of carrot aside with his spoon.

"Yeah, this kid's curry really isn't half bad...
...I just don't like carrots."

I personally thought carrots were a staple of curry.
...But to each their own.
Seeing that, Rika-chan read the name-tag on the front of his coveralls and opened her mouth.

"...That won't do, Kyouji.
You mustn't leave your carrots."

What had come over her all of a sudden...?
Everyone turned to stare at Rika-chan.
But... what Rika-chan had just done... was a critical hit.
The carrot-hating judge began stuffing his face with curry as tears streamed freely down his cheeks!

"......Kyouji.
There's seconds if you'd like some."
Uugghh — MOMMMMYYYYY!!
Crying out like that he went right on shovelling the curry in!

That's right.
...Rika-chan's curry wasn't 'simple.'
It had... the taste of a mother's cooking!!!
Even the carrot-hating judge had been won over, and Rika-chan's assessment was unshakeable.

"Ohoho.
Nobody can hold a candle to Rika's curry!!"
Satoko let out a shrill laugh and was about to declare victory — but at that moment...!
"Do not be deceived, esteemed judges!!"
"Mistaking such lowly frugality for a mother's home cooking is nothing but an illusion!!"
"What you truly wanted to eat... was this kind of curry, wasn't it?!"
Oooohhh!!!!
And there it was, plated in perfect splendour — Mion's curry!
Not only the judges, but the teacher, the underclassmen, and of course myself — we could only gasp in admiration!!
Hamburger steak in a demi-glace sauce, with a vivid fresh salad...!!
It was a set meal!!

Nothing short of a full course was worthy of the name!!
Perfect... perfectly executed!!!

"A-Amazing, amazing!!
Mii-chan, you're amazing!
Mii-chan, you're amazing!!!"
Even Satoko was struck speechless.
Even Rika-chan couldn't conceal her surprise.
The winner was as good as decided before anyone had even tasted it.
Of course the hamburger steak was hand-kneaded.
The salad dressing was homemade yet of professional quality.
And the curry itself — not even a hairsbreadth of room for criticism!!!

"This is sublime...
Truly a rich and layered flavour, Sonozaki-san!

"This curry... is the living embodiment of six thousand years of Indian wisdom fused in beauty with Japanese culinary culture...
...I am deeply moved!
This deserves full marks!!
I give it one hundred points."

"Oh, you're too kind~.
I was simply setting an example befitting a class representative."

Mion bowed graciously.
...Then winked at us with a grin!
"...I admit... defeat."

"Th-There's no way...!!
I'm certain I sabotaged that rice pot!!"
That's right.
Satoko had dumped salt into Mion's cooking canister — that should have ruined everything!!!
In that instant something clicked, and I spun around toward my own canister.
...Th-That's whHhyyy!!
At some point my canister had been opened, its contents entirely scooped out.

"M-Mion, you wretch!!
You used my rice!!!
That's not faaairrr!!
Give me half of your curry!!"
Mion clucked her tongue, wagged her finger, and let a satisfied smile spread across her face.

"Whoa there, Kei-chan — you're being soft.
You gave up at the last moment.
I didn't.
That's the difference between us.
Once you throw in the towel, it's all over!!"
Gah...!! I literally couldn't find words for my shame...!

"Mii-chan, I feel sorry for Keiichi-kun.
...Won't you share even a little...?"

"It's fine, it's fine.
Consider this tuition.
Kei-chan has just learned that you should never give up until it's all over... right?"
It galls me, but... Mion is right!
To give up on the match so easily — that was my own weakness...!!
That Mion was the one to make me see it... if anything I should be grateful to her...!
So then... even now, in this situation, I wasn't supposed to give up?!

"My curry was tipped over pot and all, and I don't even have any rice left...
How am I supposed to not lose hope in a situation like this?!"
"M-Maebara-san......"
Tomita-kun and Okamura-kun had appeared at my side.
Damn...!!
They had worked so hard for me...!!
And I had failed to live up to their expectations!!

"I-It was an accident.
...There was nothing you could do."

Both of them were crestfallen...
Gah... I couldn't accept defeat this easily.
I was their senior!!
If I didn't pick up the shattered pieces of the dream I had shown them, who would!!

I had just learned it from Mion moments ago, hadn't I?
Don't throw away the match!
Not until the final moment!

"Think, Keiichi Maebara!!
Think!!
Not about how to make curry — about how to win this contest...!!!
......Hmmmm... AaaaaaAAAAhh!!!!"
An idea came to me.
...I wasn't confident it would turn the tables, but it was better than dying on my feet!

"Tomita-kun, wash your hands and help me!!
Okamura-kun, there's something I need you to find!!"
"Understood! On it!!"
Both of them scattered to carry out their orders.

"...Oh?
Let's see how Kei-chan flails about at the end.
...Go ahead and show me."
"Nobody likes a sore loser, you know~?"

"Shut up!
Just sit there and watch my final move!!
First off, Mion — I'm taking some of your rice.
You don't get to say no!!"
"......Fine by me. It's salty though."

"It's okay if Rena helps too, right?
Keiichi-kun is in a tight spot."

"Hearing that gives me real confidence!!!
All right, Rena! Go and make some tea."
"Hau... he doesn't trust me~."

"Next, Satoko!!
Also, Rika-chan!!
I challenge you to a side wager."
"Wh-What did you saaaayyyy!?!?"
"...We'll hear your terms."
It was the one duty I owed to my two underclassmen who had sweat for my sake!

"If I receive a perfect score, Satoko, Rika-chan...
I'll be taking your curry."
"Nothing for you to eaaaaaat!!!"

"There's a limit to how far jokes should go!!!
I give that a big fat no!!!"

"...No, Satoko — it's fine.
Accept his challenge."
Patting Satoko firmly on the head, Mion spoke with all the gravitas of a club president.

"You've already dealt him a fatal blow, haven't you?
There's no way he can recover from that.
So hold your ground.
...No matter how he struggles, a comeback is impossible.
Right?"
"Mrrgh... y-yes, that's true, but......"

"...I don't mind.
Keiichi. Go for it. Yay."
The judges, growing weary of sampling so many different curries, were debating each entry.
...From what I could overhear, Mion's lead was unassailable.

"Come to think of it... what happened to Maebara-kun's curry?
I haven't tried it yet."

".........Yes.
The pot with my curry... was knocked over..."

"Hmph.
...Is that so?
What a real shame!!"

"...I may have lost the curry... but I haven't lost the contest.
...I'll have everyone singing my praises!!"
My fighting spirit drew the judges over to see what was happening...!!

"First, please just eat this without a word.
...I'll listen to your complaints afterward!
...This... is my cooking!!!!"
"...M-Maebara-kun... that's......?"

"Hmph...
...Onigiri, is it."
The judges smiled wryly at the simple offering, so at odds with the grand build-up.
Rena poured tea into the cups she had brought from the break room.

"He's... making a mockery of this!!
Look —
There's not even any filling — it's nothing more than plain salted onigiri!!!
This can't even be called a contest anymore!!!"
"......The seasoning might not be entirely off, but......"
Rena's expression told me she thought this was a clever idea, but probably still not enough to win.
"Maebara-san, is this... really enough to make a comeback!?"

"Gentlemen.
You have served me well.
...It will be all right.
Just watch."
The judges were chewing away and sipping their tea.
...No one was singing praises. No one was awestruck.
...They were simply, quietly... eating.

"......It seems Kei-chan... has learned how to use some rather underhanded tricks.
...This... is going to be difficult."

"Ah, that's right.
...Rena understands now too."

"Wh-What do you mean?!
Why is everyone so quietly... eating so much?!"

"You see, Satoko-chan.
The thing is, the judges are actually..."
...tired of eating curry!
As judges they were obliged to taste every single entry.
And since these were made by small children showing their daily gratitude, they couldn't very well eat just a single bite of each.

"Sometimes a man values quantity over taste!!
At those times, rather than rich, overwhelming flavour... simplicity is what they crave!!"

They couldn't say it aloud, but this was honestly the best thing they'd had today.
The judges confirmed it with a wry smile and a telling look in their eyes.

"I commend your effort, but... today's contest was about curry.
...I'm not sure we can properly score this."
Both the teacher and the principal folded their arms and fell to pondering.
My underclassmen heard that too... and watched the faint glimmer of hope fade away, their shoulders dropping.
Damn... was this little surprise just not enough after all...?
"...Don't give up, Kei-chan."

Mion murmured under her breath.
...That's right.
If I didn't push here, what was the point!!!

"No.
I'll have you give it a score."
"To judge curry and onigiri differently is where you're fundamentally mistaken."

"Huh?
Maebara-kun, what are you saying?
How are they the same!?"
The principal put a hand out to stop the teacher.
It seemed he was giving me one last chance.
The judges, having eaten their fill, waited quietly for my argument.

"In its homeland of India, curry is traditionally eaten with a bread called naan, isn't it?
So what we call curry rice could be said to be a Japanese adaptation — something we have adopted and rearranged."
"Just as the teacher said at the beginning, it is a fusion of Indian and Japanese culinary culture."

"I understand your point, Maebara-kun.
But what does that have to do with onigiri...?"

"It's actually very simple.
...Both curry and onigiri... are born from the wisdom of how to enjoy eating rice!!!!"
Rice was introduced to Japan from ancient China.
...Our agricultural ancestors drew water to their paddies, fighting the weather, disease, and pests to nurture the rice-eating culture that defines us...!!!

Yes — the Japanese people have devised any number of dishes, but all of them are, in the end, attempts to find ways to enjoy eating rice!!!
In other words...
"Both curry and onigiri... are the crystallisation of the same..."
"Rice culture!!!!!"

It began as a scatter and swelled into a roar.
Thundering applause broke out in my honour!!

"Enough nonsense!!!!
I refuse to accept this!!
The teachers are judging curry, not this!"

"This shouldn't even be scored!
Zero points is all it deserves!!!"

"Pl-Please, teacher...
Keiichi-kun did his very best.
Won't you acknowledge that...?"

"...This was supposed to be a curry lesson...
What should we do?"
Mion stifled a laugh through her nose and stepped forward.

"It's said that when a celebrated French chef came to Japan, the hotel staff had a great many ingredients shipped over from France for him.
But the chef didn't glance at a single one."

"...Why was that?
They were ingredients from his homeland."
"The chef made his way to the local fishing port and cooked with the fresh catch of Japan."

"Food culture is not bound by fixed rules.
...It is culture. Come to Japan, and it melds with Japanese culture to become something new entirely."
"...That is why curry and onigiri are the same — both are Japanese culture."

Mi-Mion...
She was covering for me... I was genuinely grateful.
The judges were somehow moved by this elaborate-yet-baseless piece of trivia.
A stern look settled over the teacher's face as she refolded her arms.

"...Maebara-kun, I hear your curry pot was knocked over?
Even though I said at the very beginning to be careful."
"I-I'm sorry..."

"However... as Maebara-kun and Sonozaki-san say, food culture recognises no borders.
If something has the power to move people truly, it cannot be dismissed on grounds of form alone."
"...Then? Then?"

"For spilling your pot, minus twenty points.
...But just for today...
For not giving up and pressing on, plus twenty points."
"I'll give you one hundred points."
"M-Maebara-san!! We diiiid iiiiit!!!"

My underclassmen leapt at me!!
A perfect score!!
We did it, we did it!!

The curry was spilt and our rice was taken...
But we made a comeback!!
In the end Rika-chan's curry also received full marks, so everyone except Rena had a perfect score.

...Since this had been an informal extension of club activities, there was no particular penalty game.
Rena breathed a sigh of relief.

"Now then — as promised, Satoko, Rika-chan.
I'll be confiscating your curry."
"B-But that means we won't have any lunch~! Wahhhh~~!!!"
Satoko stamped her feet in frustration, but the price of defeat was absolute.

"—Is what I wanted to say, but I'm showing mercy.
You may eat half!!"
"...Thank you, Keiichi."
My two underclassmen watched from the shadows, rivers of grateful tears streaming down their faces, exchanging a look with me that only men could understand.
W-We did it, Maebara-san!!
Yeah, it's all thanks to you both!!
We each flashed a thumbs-up!

"Now then... I'll hand this curry over to my pupils.
I've gotten pretty hungry myself.
Might as well have one of my own onigiri.
......Wait — huh?"
There was no trace of my onigiri.
The ones I had set aside for myself included.
As I peered around looking for them, the principal clapped a hand on my shoulder.

"Gahahaha!!
Listening to that speech of yours, the rice I was chewing tasted better than ever!!
Gahahahaha!!!"
"Um... could it be... that you ate my share as well...?"
"Gahahahahahaha!!!"
The principal only laughed his booming laugh and gave no answer.
Yo-You're serious...?
My eyes half-filled with tears and my stomach growling audibly, I was called over by the teacher.
"Maebara-kun, given the circumstances earlier, I gave you a perfect score... but you do understand what I mean, don't you?"
"Huh? Understand... what exactly?"
There was something strange in the teacher's eyes.
...If I had to name the colour... it was curry-coloured.

Like a pot of stewing curry, her eyes swirled in slow spirals.
A chill ran through me.
The teacher seized both my shoulders and drew me in until our noses nearly touched.

"Curry is the most venerable and sacred dish in all the world.
To place it in the same category as onigiri is something I will absolutely not allow."
"Are we clear?"

"CurryAroseinAncientIndiaAsTheFifthGreatCivilisationRivalling TheFourRiverValleyCivilisationsAndWasPraisedAtTheWorldFoodCultureExpoInKapilavastuat TheTimeOfTheBirthOfShakyamuniDuringTheReignOfKingAshokaAndMichelinWhirlWhirlEvenTheEiffelTowerWasPickledInSimmeringCurryTurmericWhetherWakingOrSleepingCurryCurryCurryCurry......"

"...Hey, Keiichi-san!
What are you stumbling around for!!
Cleaning up is part of cooking too!!"

"Keiichi-kun, your eyes look all curried?
Why is that? Why?"

Curry curry curry.
Hehehehe...♪

"Yikes... she got to him.
It's fine, he'll be back to normal by tomorrow.
Probably."
It didn't take long for the hunger pangs to bring me back to my senses.
